task_completion.description: Verify the agent accesses the correct Wazuh manager container.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal screenshot showing a command like 'docker exec -it wazuh-wazuh.manager-1' or a prompt indicating the agent is inside the 'wazuh-wazuh.manager-1' container.

task_completion.description: Verify the Wazuh manager is configured to ingest the custom JSON application log.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot of a configuration file (typically ossec.conf) showing a '<localfile>' block with '<location>/var/log/custom_webapp/app.json</location>' and '<log_format>json</log_format>'.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of a custom detection rule with the correct ID and Level.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot of a rule file (e.g., local_rules.xml) showing a rule definition starting with '<rule id="100100" level="12">'.

task_completion.description: Verify the custom rule targets the 'http_query' field for SQL injection patterns.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot of the rule definition showing a '<field name="http_query">' tag (or similar JSON field targeting) containing regex or string matching for 'UNION SELECT' or 'OR 1=1'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the system successfully triggers and records an alert for the malicious entry.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot of the Wazuh dashboard (Security events / Discover) or the terminal showing the contents of 'alerts.json'/'alerts.log' with an alert recorded for rule '100100' at level 12, containing the SQLi payload.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent modified the configuration inside the actual Wazuh container or its mapped volumes, rather than creating dummy files on the host system.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for terminal prompts, docker commands, or file paths that confirm the edits were made within the Wazuh manager environment.

integrity.description: Ensure the triggered alert is genuinely generated by the Wazuh engine and not manually typed or mocked by the agent.
integrity.visual_evidence: The alert should appear in the official Wazuh UI or contain standard Wazuh log metadata (timestamps, agent info, rule descriptions) in alerts.json, rather than being a simple echoed string.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not manually append the expected alert JSON directly into the alerts log file to bypass the detection engine.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check terminal history for commands like 'echo "{...alert...}" >> /var/ossec/logs/alerts/alerts.json'. The alert must be generated by Wazuh processing the app.json log.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target container: wazuh-wazuh.manager-1. Target log file: /var/log/custom_webapp/app.json. Expected rule ID: 100100. Expected alert level: 12. Expected SQLi patterns to detect: 'UNION SELECT' and 'OR 1=1' within the 'http_query' field. The task uses synthetically generated JSON application logs seeded into the Wazuh manager container.
